metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the Scope entry: replace `src/rebar/reducer/_cache.py` with `src/rebar/_commands/fsck.py` as the file that receives the INCOMPLETE_ROLLBACK detection rule (`_check_snapshot` / `_scan`) and repair routing (`_repair_plan` / `_repair_ticket`).
findings.finding: `src/rebar/reducer/_cache.py` is listed in Scope as the edit target for 'fsck repair path (mixed-state recognition)', but that file contains only cache I/O utilities (RETIRED_SUFFIX, is_active_event, read_cache, write_cache, prepare_event_files) — no detection or repair logic. The INCOMPLETE_ROLLBACK detection must land in `src/rebar/_commands/fsck.py` (_check_snapshot, _scan, _repair_plan).
findings.location: Scope section: `src/rebar/reducer/_cache.py` / `fsck repair path (mixed-state recognition)`
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope bullet, change `src/rebar/reducer/_cache.py / fsck repair path (mixed-state recognition)` to `src/rebar/_commands/fsck.py (_check_snapshot, _scan, _repair_plan, _repair_ticket — INCOMPLETE_ROLLBACK detection and repair routing)`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Tighten the INCOMPLETE_ROLLBACK detection rule: the proposed condition ('snapshot present AND ≥1 UUID resolves only to *.retired') fires on every healthy compacted ticket; clarify whether detection targets only the partial case (snapshot present AND ≥1 source UUID still active as .json AND ≥1 source UUID only as .retired), and document how the all-reverses-failed degenerate case is handled (it is undetectable by file-state alone without an out-of-band marker).
findings.finding: The plan's INCOMPLETE_ROLLBACK detection rule — 'snapshot present AND ≥1 source_event_uuids resolves only to a *.retired file' — is satisfied by every healthy, fully-compacted ticket (all sources are *.retired-only after a successful compaction), so the rule as stated will generate false positives on every already-compacted ticket and cannot distinguish the incomplete-rollback state from normal post-compaction state.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3 (fsck INCOMPLETE_ROLLBACK detection); What section — 'INCOMPLETE_ROLLBACK: a snapshot file is present AND at least one UUID in its source_event_uuids resolves only to a *.retired file'
findings.suggested_fix: Narrow the fsck detection rule to the *partial* rollback failure case: 'snapshot present AND ≥1 source UUID still active as .json (SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT already covers this) AND ≥1 source UUID only as .retired'. The all-reverses-failed degenerate case (identical to clean compaction state) can be accepted as undetectable without a sentinel, or a lightweight sentinel file (e.g. .compact-rollback-incomplete) can be written during the rollback failure path to give fsck an unambiguous signal.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly specify that the fsck --repair-snapshots rebuild trigger (currently `any('SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT' in f or 'ORPHAN_EVENT' in f ...)`) must be extended to also trigger on INCOMPLETE_ROLLBACK findings, so AC4's repair path actually fires for the new issue kind.
findings.finding: The fsck --repair-snapshots branch currently gates rebuilds on 'SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT' or 'ORPHAN_EVENT' appearing in findings (fsck.py line 191: `rebuildable = any('SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT' in f or 'ORPHAN_EVENT' in f for f in findings)`). The plan extends fsck with INCOMPLETE_ROLLBACK but does not specify that this trigger condition must also accept INCOMPLETE_ROLLBACK findings, so the repair path would silently skip the new issue kind.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4 ('fsck --repair-snapshots rebuilds from the retained snapshot + residual *.retired sources'); fsck.py lines 191-202
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the What/Scope section: 'Update the rebuildable trigger in fsck._scan to include INCOMPLETE_ROLLBACK alongside SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT and ORPHAN_EVENT.'

findings.finding: `src/rebar/reducer/_cache.py` is named as the edit target for 'fsck repair path (mixed-state recognition)', but this file contains only cache read/write/hash logic with no repair or detection code. All fsck repair logic (`_repair_plan`, `_repair_ticket`, `_scan`, `_check_snapshot`) lives in `src/rebar/_commands/fsck.py`. The incomplete-rollback mixed-state detection and repair must be added to `fsck.py`, not `_cache.py`.
findings.location: Scope: `src/rebar/reducer/_cache.py` / fsck repair path (mixed-state recognition)
findings.suggested_fix: Change the Scope line to: `src/rebar/_commands/fsck.py` (mixed-state detection in `_repair_plan` / `_check_snapshot`; retain `src/rebar/reducer/_cache.py` only if adding a new exported constant like `INCOMPLETE_ROLLBACK_SENTINEL`).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `_repair_plan`, `_repair_ticket`, and `_repair_run` in `src/rebar/_commands/fsck.py` to the Scope, and specify how INCOMPLETE_ROLLBACK maps into the repair plan schema (new key, or reuse of `auto_orphans` / `retire` semantics).
findings.finding: `_repair_plan` and `_repair_run` in `src/rebar/_commands/fsck.py` are the actual dispatch functions that must be extended for AC4 (`fsck --repair-snapshots` rebuilds from retained snapshot + residual `*.retired` sources), but they are not named in the Scope. The current `_repair_plan` dict schema has three keys (`retire`, `auto_orphans`, `triage_orphans`); `INCOMPLETE_ROLLBACK` is a distinct fourth category that must either map to an existing key or add a new one, with corresponding changes to `_repair_run`'s `flagged` filter.
findings.location: Scope section; src/rebar/_commands/fsck.py lines 330-439 (_repair_plan, _repair_ticket, _repair_run)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact integration point for `INCOMPLETE_ROLLBACK` in the `--repair-snapshots` path: either extend `rebuildable` in `_scan` to also match `'INCOMPLETE_ROLLBACK'` findings, or add an `incomplete_rollback` bucket to `_repair_plan`/`_repair_ticket`, and document which approach is chosen.
findings.finding: The plan specifies that `fsck --repair-snapshots` handles `INCOMPLETE_ROLLBACK` by rebuilding from the retained snapshot + residual `*.retired` sources, but never specifies how `INCOMPLETE_ROLLBACK` integrates into the `_repair_plan` / `rebuildable` detection gate — the existing `rebuildable` check only recognises `'SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT'` and `'ORPHAN_EVENT'` substrings, so `INCOMPLETE_ROLLBACK` findings would silently bypass the repair path.
findings.location: ## What — 'fsck --repair-snapshots treats INCOMPLETE_ROLLBACK by rebuilding…' / AC bullet 4 / src/rebar/_commands/fsck.py:191 (rebuildable gate)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Strengthen AC2's data-integrity assertion to a specific observable check (e.g., 'replay from the retained snapshot + *.retired sources yields a state deep-equal to pre-compaction, and a follow-up `fsck` is clean') matching AC1's precision.
findings.finding: AC2's data-integrity assertion 'no source data lost' is not a specific, observer-verifiable criterion. Unlike AC1 which specifies 'reduced state deep-equal to pre-compaction, clean fsck, and idempotent re-compaction', AC2 leaves the implementer without a concrete way to verify data integrity in the reverse-rename-fault scenario — a replay-equal comparison, file-content hash, or event-count check would make this observable.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Reverse-rename fault test (AC2)
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'no source data lost' in AC2 with: 'replaying from the retained snapshot + residual *.retired sources yields a reduced state deep-equal to pre-compaction state; a subsequent `fsck` reports INCOMPLETE_ROLLBACK and `fsck --repair-snapshots` converges to a clean `fsck`.'

findings.finding: AC2 asserts 'no source data lost' while simultaneously permitting 'some *.retired sources not restored', but provides no concrete check that the retained snapshot actually covers those unreachable *.retired files — the assertion is stated as policy, not as a measurable test outcome.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC2 (Reverse-rename fault test)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify (in the INCOMPLETE_ROLLBACK detection rule) how fsck distinguishes between a genuine incomplete-rollback state and the intermediate state produced by an in-progress or interrupted fsck --repair-snapshots (e.g., by checking for the .snapshot-rebuild.bak sentinel or by requiring the INCOMPLETE_ROLLBACK condition to fire only on snapshots that are NOT themselves the most-recently-written snapshot when a .bak is present), and assert the store state after a partially-completed repair is correctly detected and re-converges on a second run.
findings.finding: The INCOMPLETE_ROLLBACK detection rule as specified ('snapshot present AND ≥1 UUID in source_event_uuids resolves only to a *.retired file') is indistinguishable from the intermediate state produced by fsck --repair-snapshots itself: the rebuild writes a NEW snapshot whose source_event_uuids intentionally include the .retired sources, but before those .retired files are cleaned up (or the old retained snapshot is retired), a re-entrant fsck would see the new snapshot satisfying the same rule, potentially re-triggering or looping. The plan does not assert what the store state looks like after a partially-completed fsck repair, nor that a second fsck run on a partially-repaired store converges correctly.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'fsck (no flag) reports a distinct INCOMPLETE_ROLLBACK finding' and 'fsck --repair-snapshots rebuilds from the retained snapshot + residual *.retired sources'

findings.finding: The plan's AC3 asserts 'fsck deterministically detects and repairs the incomplete-rollback state, including the inner reverse-rename-failure sub-case (snapshot retained),' but the existing fsck code only triggers the repair path when SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT or ORPHAN_EVENT is found. In the degenerate all-reverses-failed sub-case (zero source files restored to .json, all remaining as .retired), no active .json source files exist to trigger SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT, and the retained snapshot's source UUIDs all point at .retired files that _check_snapshot excludes (the .json extension filter at fsck.py line 279 means .json.retired is already invisible). The code therefore emits no finding and fsck exits 0 — leaving the mixed state silently undetected.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3; src/rebar/_commands/fsck.py lines 278–304
findings.suggested_fix: In `_check_snapshot` (or a new dedicated check in `_scan`), after computing `event_files` (active-only), count how many `source_uuids` appear in neither `event_files` (active) nor any `.retired` file. If all are in `.retired` and the snapshot is present, emit an `INCOMPLETE_ROLLBACK` or similar tag. Alternatively, extend the `_repair_plan` heuristic to detect a snapshot-present + all-sources-retired state and classify it as needing a rebuild.

findings.finding: AC3 requires fsck to 'deterministically detect and repair the incomplete-rollback state, including the inner reverse-rename-failure sub-case (snapshot retained),' but the plan does not specify what new detection rule or issue kind fsck uses for this state. The existing --repair-snapshots path triggers only on SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT / ORPHAN_EVENT, neither of which fires when stranded .retired files are excluded from the active-event scan by is_active_event — so the sub-case is silently undetected by the current fsck logic.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3; src/rebar/_commands/fsck.py lines 191-205 (_scan Check 4), lines 278-321 (_check_snapshot)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a new fsck Check 4b: for each ticket, if a SNAPSHOT exists and any source UUID maps to a `.retired` file that has no active `.json` counterpart AND the non-retired path also does not exist as an active file, emit `INCOMPLETE_ROLLBACK: {ticket_id} — {n} source(s) stranded as *.retired after failed rollback`. Hook `_repair_plan` to handle `INCOMPLETE_ROLLBACK` via `rebuild_snapshot_from_full_log`.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly specify what `fsck --repair-snapshots` does with the residual `*.retired` files after converging (e.g., rename back to active filenames, delete, or leave-as-is with rationale).
findings.finding: The RETIRE/cleanup path for *.retired source files after `fsck --repair-snapshots` converges is not specified: the plan says 'rebuilding from the retained snapshot + residual *.retired sources … converging to a consistent state' but does not state whether the *.retired files are renamed back to active, deleted, or left as-is, risking indefinite accumulation of invisible-to-replay files.
findings.location: ## What — fsck --repair-snapshots; Acceptance Criteria AC4
findings.suggested_fix: After a successful rebuild, rename each *.retired file back to its canonical (un-retired) filename so the store is in the same state as a clean post-compaction result, then remove the retained snapshot. Document this in the plan's 'What' section and add an assertion in AC4 that no *.retired files remain after a successful repair.

findings.finding: The plan asserts (in the 'What' section) 'emit an explicit `rollback incomplete; run fsck` diagnostic' as a new behaviour to add in compact.py, but the current rollback path already logs a warning via `logger.warning` (compact.py line 238–241) and writes `sys.stderr.write('Error: failed to retire folded events while holding lock\n')` (line 246). The plan does not clarify whether the proposed new diagnostic is the stderr line, a new structured sentinel file, or an additional signal — leaving it ambiguous whether the current stderr output satisfies the AC or new work is genuinely required.
findings.location: What section — 'emit an explicit rollback incomplete; run fsck diagnostic'; src/rebar/_commands/compact.py lines 235–247
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC2: 'assert stderr contains the literal string "rollback incomplete" and the snapshot path' (or equivalent) so the test is deterministic and the implementer knows what to write.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a documentation update (ADR, CLAUDE.md, or inline design note in the affected modules) capturing the retain-snapshot-on-rollback-failure invariant and the INCOMPLETE_ROLLBACK detection/repair contract between compact.py and fsck.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new cross-component recovery contract (retain-snapshot-on-rollback-failure in compact.py + INCOMPLETE_ROLLBACK detection/repair in fsck/cache.py) that is a novel invariant for the system, but no update to any ADR, CLAUDE.md, or design doc is specified to capture this decision for future maintainers.
findings.location: ## What — 'Chosen approach (committed — not "retain OR sentinel")' and fsck INCOMPLETE_ROLLBACK rule
findings.suggested_fix: Add a brief ADR or update CLAUDE.md with: (1) the invariant 'on any reverse-rename failure, the snapshot MUST be retained', (2) the INCOMPLETE_ROLLBACK definition and its distinction from SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT/ORPHAN_EVENT, and (3) the repair path via fsck --repair-snapshots. Alternatively, add a module-level docstring to compact.py's rollback block and to the fsck INCOMPLETE_ROLLBACK rule cross-referencing each other.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `_STRUCTURED_KINDS` in `src/rebar/_commands/fsck.py` to the Scope (or note in the plan that `incomplete_rollback` must be added to it so `--output json` emits ticket_id/filename fields for the new finding).
findings.finding: `_STRUCTURED_KINDS` in `src/rebar/_commands/fsck.py` (line 35) controls which issue kinds get ticket_id/filename fields in `--output json` output via `_transform_json`. The new `INCOMPLETE_ROLLBACK` kind is not listed in Scope or the plan's edits, so it will be emitted as an unstructured detail string in JSON mode, silently degrading machine-readable output for the new finding.
findings.location: src/rebar/_commands/fsck.py lines 35-42 (`_STRUCTURED_KINDS`); Scope section (unenumerated)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/_cli/help/fsck.txt`, `tests/golden/cli_help/fsck__help.out`, `tests/golden/cli_help/fsck__dashhelp.out`, and `docs/cli-reference.md` to the Scope as files that must be updated if `--repair-snapshots` help text changes.
findings.finding: The fsck help text is byte-parity pinned by `tests/interfaces/facades/test_cli_argparse.py` against `src/rebar/_cli/help/fsck.txt`, `tests/golden/cli_help/fsck__help.out`, `tests/golden/cli_help/fsck__dashhelp.out`, and `docs/cli-reference.md`. If the plan extends `--repair-snapshots` to handle `INCOMPLETE_ROLLBACK` (AC4) or changes the description, these four files must be updated, but none are listed in Scope.
findings.location: Scope section (unenumerated consumers); tests/interfaces/facades/test_cli_argparse.py; tests/golden/cli_help/fsck__help.out; src/rebar/_cli/help/fsck.txt; docs/cli-reference.md

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify whether `INCOMPLETE_ROLLBACK` should be added to `_STRUCTURED_KINDS` in `fsck.py` and, if so, define the expected `ticket_id`/`detail` decomposition for its JSON output format.
findings.finding: `INCOMPLETE_ROLLBACK` is a new fsck issue kind the plan introduces but never adds to `_STRUCTURED_KINDS`, the dispatch table that controls whether `_transform_json` emits a structured JSON object (with `ticket_id`/`filename`/`detail` keys) versus a flat `detail` string for that kind.
findings.location: ## What — 'Extend fsck with a detection rule…named issue kind INCOMPLETE_ROLLBACK' / src/rebar/_commands/fsck.py:35-42 (_STRUCTURED_KINDS)
findings.suggested_fix: Add `'incomplete_rollback'` to `_STRUCTURED_KINDS` in `fsck.py` (matching the lowercase convention used in `_transform_json`), and emit the line as `INCOMPLETE_ROLLBACK: {ticket_id}/{snapshot_filename} — {detail}` so the ticket_id and detail fields are populated in JSON output.

findings.finding: The plan presents two structurally distinct mechanisms ('retain the snapshot OR write a recovery sentinel') in the What section and AC2 without selecting one, and provides no positive rationale for whichever approach is adopted. These differ materially: retaining the existing snapshot requires no new file and leverages the existing SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT fsck check, while a recovery sentinel requires a new file format, new fsck detection logic, and new repair path. Without a committed choice and rationale, the implementer faces an open design decision at coding time, and the AC2 fsck coverage requirement changes depending on which branch is taken.
findings.location: What section ('retain it (or write a recovery sentinel)') and AC2 ('The chosen policy for the residual mixed state … is documented')
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'retain it (or write a recovery sentinel)' with 'retain it — the snapshot already carries the full folded state, so no event data is lost; the existing fsck SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT detection covers the case where un-reversed sources remain as active .json files (if any), and .retired files that failed to un-retire are already invisible to normal replay and covered by the snapshot.' Update AC2 to remove the open policy question and confirm that no new fsck artifact class is introduced.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State in AC1 what 'deep-equal to pre-compaction' means concretely — e.g. 'assert the set of event UUIDs and their content (loaded via the normal replay path) matches a snapshot captured before compaction ran' — so the agent can write a deterministic assertion.
findings.finding: AC1 asserts 'reduced state deep-equal to pre-compaction' but provides no concrete proving command or comparison method — an agent must invent the assertion strategy (compare event UUIDs? JSON diff? replay output?), which is a defaultable gap that may produce a weak or incorrect assertion.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1 (Forward-rename fault test)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a parenthetical to AC1: '…reduced state deep-equal to pre-compaction (assert `set(event.uuid for event in replay()) == pre_uuids` and each event's payload hash matches)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit note under AC5 (or per AC 1–4) that `make test` is the proving command for all behavioral ACs, and that the new tests must be placed in `tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_compact_negative.py` (already named in Scope) so they are auto-discovered.
findings.finding: ACs 1–4 have no explicit proving command; `make test` (AC5) implicitly covers them only if the new tests are correctly wired into the test suite, but the plan never states this connection — an agent might treat AC5 as a separate lint gate and omit verifying ACs 1–4 individually.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — ACs 1–4 and AC5
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC5: 'Note: `make test` serves as the proving command for ACs 1–4 because the new fault-injection tests in `tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_compact_negative.py` are auto-discovered by pytest; confirm test count increases by ≥2 over the baseline.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief description in the 'What' section (or inline in AC1) of what 'idempotent re-compaction' means and confirm it requires no new implementation work (i.e., it is a pure test assertion on existing behavior), so the criterion is unambiguously verifiable.
findings.finding: AC1 asserts 'an idempotent re-compaction' as a success condition, but the plan body ('What', 'Why', 'Scope') never describes any work to ensure or verify re-compaction idempotency after a successfully-rolled-back forward fault — this condition has no corresponding described deliverable or implementation note.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1 (Forward-rename fault test), last sub-assertion
findings.suggested_fix: In AC1, replace 'and an idempotent re-compaction' with 'and re-running compaction on the restored state succeeds and produces an output identical to the original compaction (verified by deep-equal of the resulting snapshot)' — or, if this requires no new code, add a parenthetical in the 'What' section noting that re-compaction idempotency is an emergent property of the successful rollback, tested but not separately implemented.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify whether `compact` detects an existing orphaned snapshot (INCOMPLETE_ROLLBACK state) at startup and either refuses with a clear error ('run fsck --repair-snapshots first') or delegates to the repair path before proceeding.
findings.finding: The lifecycle of the retained snapshot when `compact` is re-run in INCOMPLETE_ROLLBACK state (before `fsck --repair-snapshots`) is unspecified: the plan only routes detection/repair through `fsck`, but does not say whether `compact` itself detects a pre-existing orphaned snapshot and refuses or warns. A user who re-runs compact after a partial-rollback failure may trigger a second compaction that conflicts with or silently overwrites the retained snapshot.
findings.location: ## What — 'retain the snapshot' approach; Scope: src/rebar/_commands/compact.py
findings.suggested_fix: Add a precondition check at the top of compact's main path: scan for any snapshot whose source_event_uuids include only *.retired counterparts, and if found, exit with a non-zero code and the message 'Store is in INCOMPLETE_ROLLBACK state; run fsck --repair-snapshots before compacting.'

findings.finding: AC2 states 'the snapshot-present skip must tolerate residual *.retired files so no committed state regresses' as a required policy, but does not specify which component owns this rule, whether it already holds (compact.py line 222-223 already skips existing .retired targets), or whether changes to _cache.py's prepare_event_files or the reducer are needed — leaving the processing rule unanchored.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2; src/rebar/_commands/compact.py lines 222-223; src/rebar/reducer/_cache.py prepare_event_files

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
